title: Lynette Schwanger
text:
Data Science

  

    
      Quarto Report
      Exploring Streamflow Across the Colorado River Basin
      Built a complete analytical pipeline using 44 years of USGS streamflow data, including low-flow threshold monitoring, basin-wide drought visualization, and a predictive model of annual flow at the Colorado River Compact's primary accounting point.
      Read Report →
    

    
      Quarto Report
      The Geometry of Place: Spatial Analysis and Distance Calculations
      Computed distances from 28,000 US cities to national and state borders, quantified the population living within the ACLU's 100-mile border zone, and built an interactive Leaflet web map with toggleable layers and popups.
      Read Report →
    

    
      Quarto Report
      National Dam Inventory: Tessellations and Spatial Clustering
      Compared five tessellation methods to demonstrate how the Modifiable Areal Unit Problem shapes spatial analysis, mapped dam distributions by purpose across the US, and computed local spatial autocorrelation to identify regional hotspots and coldspots.
      Read Report →
    

    
      Quarto Report
      Flood Mapping with Landsat Remote Sensing: Palo, Iowa
      Accessed Landsat 8 imagery via the Microsoft Planetary Computer STAC API, applied five spectral water indices and unsupervised k-means classification to map 2016 Cedar River flood extents, and compared methods through an aggregated uncertainty map.
      Read Report →

title: Resume
text:
⬇ Download PDF
    
  

  
  
    Summary
    
      Graduate student in Ecosystem Science and Sustainability with a focus on water resources and carbon management.
      Integrating a proven background in business, media, and program leadership to bridge science and strategy.
      Passionate about transforming complex environmental data into actionable solutions that advance climate resilience.
    
    Core Competencies
    
      Data Management & Analysis
      Environmental Policy Interpretation
      Organizational Agility
      Translating Complex Analyses for Broad Audiences
      Stakeholder Engagement & Collaboration
      Strategic Project Development & Management
    
  

  
  
    Water Resources & Environmental Data Science

    
      Water Database Specialist
      March 2026 – Present
      Radical Open Science Syndicate · Fort Collins, CO
      
        Develop geospatial water supply databases for a National Park Service climate adaptation project
        Apply QA/QC workflows to improve data accuracy, consistency, and usability
        Automate data querying and visualization tasks using R scripting to improve efficiency and reproducibility
        Collaborate with NPS staff to streamline database access and support practical use by end users
      
    

    
      Assistant Instructor - Environmental Data Science Applications: Introduction
      January 2026 – Present
      Colorado State University · Fort Collins, CO
      
        Selected as Assistant Instructor after completing the course based on academic performance
        Facilitate weekly office hours providing R troubleshooting and data analysis support to students
      
    
  

  
  
    Selected Projects — Colorado State University

    
      Reproducible Remote Sensing Model - Restored Wetland Photosynthetic Recovery
      Spring 2026
      
        Built and validated a predictive model of wetland gross primary production (GPP) using HLS remote sensing data, spectral indices, and AmeriFlux ground-truth observations
        Applied the model across restored wetland sites on Twitchell Island to assess recovery trajectories and compare seasonal carbon uptake patterns
      
    

    
      R Shiny Application - Interactive Fen and Wetland Map
      Fall 2025
      
        Built an interactive Shiny application comparing three Colorado wetland and fen datasets with polygon-level queries to support transparent, data-informed environmental planning
      
    

    
      Colorado Water Planning and Governance - Anti-Speculation Doctrine Case Analysis
      Fall 2025
      
        Analyzed a Colorado Supreme Court case on municipal water supply planning, demonstrating how realistic demand projections and the "can and will" test help protect shared water resources
        Summarized takeaways for mountain communities managing growth amid climate impacts on water resources
      
    

    
      Policy Brief - Fen Protection via Regulation 87
      Fall 2025
      
        Reviewed regulations and authored a policy brief to Colorado's Water Quality Control Commission advocating for stronger fen protections to safeguard water quality, drought buffering, fire resiliency, and carbon storage
        Recommended actionable steps including avoidance standards, stewardship measures, and improved fen mapping
      
    
  

  
  
    Professional Experience

    
      CEO | Founder | Partner
      January 2019 – May 2025
      Lightswitch Video · Leadville, CO (Remote/Hybrid)
      
        Led stakeholder and client meetings, translating technical needs into clear deliverables and timelines
        Fostered strategic partnerships and negotiated contracts to support organizational growth
        Directly grew Equinix account by 250% within four years
        Increased company net worth from $56,879 to over $1.8 million
        Cultivated a values-driven company culture prioritizing inclusivity and collaboration
      
    

    
      Video Production Manager
      May 2013 – January 2019
      Lightswitch Video
      
        Built lasting client relationships with organizations such as Chicago Tribune and Gannett
        Hired and supervised contract teams to deliver high-quality video content for clients
        Improved workflows through continuous process assessment
      
    

    
      Video Production Coordinator
      November 2011 – May 2013
      Lightswitch Video
      
        Established the Video Operations department, creating efficient workflows to scale operations
        Collaborated across departments to improve digital infrastructure and user experience
      
    
  

  
  
    Education

    
      Professional Science Master's - Ecosystem Science and Sustainability
      Expected December 2026
      Colorado State University · Fort Collins, CO
      
        Graduate Certificates in Water Resources and Carbon Management | GPA: 4.0
      
    

    
      Bachelor of Arts - English Creative Writing & Cinema
      May 2009
      Denison University · Granville, OH
      
        Magna Cum Laude, Dean's List, Honors Program | GPA: 3.78
      
    
  

  
  
    Volunteer Work

    
      Secretary and Board of Directors Member
      January 2026 – Present
      Lake County Search and Rescue
      
        Elected to the Board of Directors as Secretary
        Track team certifications, attendance, and new member requirements
        Set agendas for monthly meetings and record minutes
      
    

    
      Search and Rescue Volunteer
      May 2024 – Present
      Lake County Search and Rescue
      
        Provide emergency response support, delivering safe and efficient outcomes for those in need
        Apply skills in navigation, risk assessment, and incident command in challenging mountain environments
      
    

    
      Stream Restoration Volunteer
      October 2025
      Wildlands Restoration Volunteers
      
        Built beaver dam analogs and post-assisted log structures to restore floodplain connectivity and wetland habitat

title: Lynette Schwanger
text:
Lynette Schwanger
    Environmental ScientistWater Resources · Carbon Management

title: Lynette Schwanger
text:
About
I am a graduate student in Ecosystem Science and Sustainability at Colorado State University, focused on water resources and carbon management. My work centers on translating complex environmental data into actionable solutions, whether that’s building geospatial water supply databases for a National Park Service climate adaptation project, developing remote sensing models to assess wetland carbon recovery, or teaching environmental data science to the next cohort of students at CSU.
I came to this field through an unconventional path. After over a decade building and leading a video production company, a long-term partnership with a client in the tech industry began shifting my focus. Watching growing energy demands accelerate environmental pressure made me want to be on the other side of that problem. But it was a moment on Twin Lakes as I was ferrying firefighters across the water toward the smoke of the Interlaken Fire, watching the flames threaten a reservoir my community depended on, that made my path clear. I’ve centered my graduate work on data-informed approaches to climate resilience and water resource protection, bringing my skills in stakeholder engagement, strategic thinking, and clear communication to the problems that matter most to me and my home community in Leadville, Colorado.
